text: You never really understand a person until you consider things from his point of view—until you climb into his skin and walk around in it.
I wanted you to see what real courage is, instead of getting the idea that courage is a man with a gun in his hand.
context: Courage as endurance for a lost cause.

text: The one thing that doesn't abide by majority rule is a person's conscience.
Shoot all the bluejays you want, if you can hit 'em, but remember it's a sin to kill a mockingbird.
But there is one way in this country in which all men are created equal—that institution, gentlemen, is a court.
Before I can live with other folks I've got to live with myself.
The one place where a man ought to get a square deal is in a courtroom, be he any color of the rainbow, but people have a way of carrying their resentments right into a jury box.
It's when you know you're licked before you begin but you begin anyway and you see it through no matter what.
